Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a student user in the Navodaya Memories yearbook, storing their profile information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Student entity. This ID typically comes from an external authentication system.

batchId.description: Reference to the Batch the student belongs to. (Relationship: Batch 1:N Student)

name.description: The full name of the student.

email.description: The email address of the student, used for identification (not authentication).

profilePhotoUrl.description: URL to the student's profile picture.

shortBio.description: A brief, introductory biography of the student, suitable for card views.

fullBio.description: A comprehensive biography providing more details about the student, displayed on their detailed profile page.

Batch.title: Batch
Batch.description: Represents a group of students belonging to a specific academic batch or graduation period.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Batch entity.

name.description: The name of the batch, e.g., 'Batch 2018-2025'.

startYear.description: The academic year when the batch started their journey (e.g., Class 6).

endYear.description: The academic year when the batch concluded their journey (e.g., Class 12).

Memory.title: Memory
Memory.description: A personal recollection or story contributed by a student, detailing a specific event or experience.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Memory entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student who contributed this memory. (Relationship: Student 1:N Memory)

title.description: A concise title for the memory.

description.description: The detailed text content of the memory.

classYearLabel.description: The academic class year this memory pertains to (e.g., 'Class 6', 'Class 12').

eventDate.description: The specific date when the remembered event occurred.

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the memory was uploaded to the platform.

photoIds.description: References to Photos associated with this specific memory. (Relationship: Memory 1:N Photo, a memory can have multiple photos).

Quote.title: Quote
Quote.description: A significant quote, often personal or inspirational, attributed to a student.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quote entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student to whom this quote is attributed. (Relationship: Student 1:N Quote)

text.description: The full text of the quote.

author.description: The person who originally said or wrote the quote. Could be the student or someone else.

context.description: Optional context or background information for the quote.

Highlight.title: Highlight
Highlight.description: A noteworthy achievement, event, or experience of a student during their academic journey.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Highlight entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student to whom this highlight belongs. (Relationship: Student 1:N Highlight)

title.description: A concise title for the highlight or achievement.

description.description: Detailed description of the highlight, achievement, or significant event.

classYearLabel.description: The academic class year this highlight pertains to (e.g., 'Class 6', 'Class 12').

date.description: The date when the highlight occurred.

Photo.title: Photo
Photo.description: An image asset, potentially linked to a memory or student profile, stored for the gallery.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Photo entity.

url.description: The URL where the image file is stored.

caption.description: A brief caption or description for the photo.

uploadedByStudentId.description: Reference to the Student who uploaded this photo. (Relationship: Student 1:N Photo)

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the photo was uploaded.

definition.description: Collection for academic batches, defining a group of students for a specific period. This data is generally public for authenticated users.

params.name: batchId
params.description: Unique identifier for the academic batch.

definition.description: Main collection for student profiles. The document ID (`studentId`) matches the Firebase Authentication UID. Includes denormalized 'batchId' for efficient batch-specific student queries.

params.name: studentId
params.description: Unique identifier for the student, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Subcollection under a student's profile storing their personal memories. Includes denormalized 'studentId' for authorization independence.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the student who owns this memory.

params.name: memoryId
params.description: Unique identifier for the specific memory.

definition.description: Subcollection under a student's profile storing their attributed quotes. Includes denormalized 'studentId' for authorization independence.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the student to whom this quote is attributed.

params.name: quoteId
params.description: Unique identifier for the specific quote.

definition.description: Subcollection under a student's profile storing their highlights and achievements. Includes denormalized 'studentId' for authorization independence.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the student to whom this highlight belongs.

params.name: highlightId
params.description: Unique identifier for the specific highlight.

definition.description: Subcollection under a student's profile for photos they have uploaded. Includes denormalized 'uploadedByStudentId' for authorization independence.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the student who uploaded this photo.

params.name: photoId
params.description: Unique identifier for the specific photo.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable, adhering strictly to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence:** This is primarily achieved by structuring student-owned data (Memories, Quotes, Highlights, Photos) as subcollections directly under the respective `student` document (e.g., `/students/{studentId}/memories/{memoryId}`). Each document in these subcollections explicitly includes a denormalized `studentId` (or `uploadedByStudentId` for photos) field. This allows security rules to evaluate ownership simply by comparing `request.auth.uid` with the `{studentId}` wildcard in the path and the `studentId` field within the document itself, without needing any expensive or complex `get()` operations on parent documents. The `batchId` is denormalized directly onto the `Student` document, allowing for batch-specific queries without traversing parent hierarchies.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structure facilitates secure `list` operations through consistent structural segregation and explicit ownership. All documents within a given collection (e.g., `/students`, `/batches`) or subcollection (e.g., `/students/{studentId}/memories`) share a homogeneous security posture. 
*   For `batches`, read access is granted to all authenticated users, making batch listings straightforward. 
*   For `students`, the primary collection `/students/{studentId}` allows for direct queries (e.g., to list all students in a specific batch using `where('batchId', '==', batchId)`). Read access to student profiles and their subcollections (Memories, Quotes, Highlights, Photos) is granted to all authenticated users, consistent with a yearbook concept where profiles are public within the app. Write access to a student's profile and their personal content is strictly limited to the owning student (`request.auth.uid == studentId`). 
*   By denormalizing the `studentId` into subcollection documents and leveraging path-based ownership, rules can guarantee that `list` operations only return data that the user is authorized to see based on the query, rather than filtering results post-query, thus fulfilling the QAPs principle.

This design prioritizes clear ownership, eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, and supports efficient querying and rule evaluation, making the system robust and easy to maintain.
